title: Gulliver's Travels into Several Remote Nations of the World
author: Swift, Jonathan
summary: Lemuel Gulliver, a surgeon and voyager, narrates travels prepared for publication by his cousin Sympson. After study at Emanuel and Leyden and service on ships including the Swallow and Antelope, he recounts voyages to strange lands. The publisher frames the papers; Gulliver’s letter protests editorial cuts, defends veracity, and complains that his satire did not reform English manners. He describes humanlike Yahoos and rational Houyhnhnms, political innuendo and domestic details: marriage to Mary Burton, masters Bates and Prichard, and retirement near Newark. The opening chapter begins Gulliver’s fourth sea voyage, ending with a shipwreck en route to the East Indies.

characters.name: Lemuel Gulliver
characters.personality: observant, earnest, candid narrator with satirical purpose
characters.lifespan: survives voyages; retired near Newark; keeps two degenerate Houyhnhnms in his stable (per letter)

characters.name: Richard Sympson
characters.lifespan: alive; holds custody of Gulliver's papers and published them

characters.name: Captain William Prichard
characters.personality: ship captain, master of the Antelope
characters.lifespan: commanded the Antelope on voyage from Bristol (May 4, 1699)

characters.name: Captain Abraham Pannel
characters.personality: ship captain, earlier employer of Gulliver
characters.lifespan: master of the Swallow during Gulliver's earlier service

characters.name: Mr. James Bates
characters.lifespan: died two years after Gulliver's marriage (stated in text)

characters.name: Mrs. Mary Burton
characters.lifespan: married Gulliver and provided a portion; alive at time of earlier events

characters.name: Mr. Edmund Burton
characters.lifespan: mentioned as guardian of family funds; fate not specified

characters.name: Uncle John
characters.lifespan: assisted Gulliver financially; further fate not given

characters.name: Cousin Dampier
characters.lifespan: referenced as example for arranging travel writings

characters.name: Queen Anne
characters.personality: revered in text ('most pious and glorious memory')

characters.name: Lord of Godolphin
characters.lifespan: mentioned as minister during Queen Anne's reign; fate not given

characters.lifespan: inhabit Houyhnhnmland; central to Gulliver's later reflections

characters.name: The Yahoos
characters.lifespan: present in Gulliver's accounts and used as a foil to Houyhnhnms

characters.name: Two degenerate Houyhnhnms
characters.personality: less perfect examples of their kind, instructive
characters.lifespan: kept by Gulliver in his stable; source of some comfort

characters.name: Gulliver's father
characters.personality: landed small estate owner in Nottinghamshire
characters.lifespan: supported Gulliver's early education; further fate unspecified

characters.name: Gulliver's four brothers
characters.lifespan: mentioned only as siblings; no further fate given

characters.name: The printer
characters.personality: careless (said to have confounded times and dates)
characters.lifespan: responsible for errors in publication according to Gulliver

characters.name: The carrier
characters.lifespan: used to send libels and pamphlets weekly (referenced only)
